Isolation Comments: isolated from the human gut encodes resistance to all tetracyclines tested, including eravacycline and omadacycline
Additional Metadata: collected_by:Atta ur Rahman School of Applied Biosciences, National University of Sciences and Technology, Islamabad, Pakistan
Comments: Tetracycline resistance by antibiotic inactivation was first identified in commensal organisms but has since been reported in environmental and pathogenic microbes. Here, we identify and characterize an expanded pool of tet(X)-like genes in environmental and human commensal metagenomes via inactivation by antibiotic selection of metagenomic libraries. These genes formed two distinct clades according to habitat of origin, and resistance phenotypes were similarly correlated. Each gene isolated from the human gut encodes resistance to all tetracyclines tested, including eravacycline and omadacycline. We report a biochemical and structural characterization of one enzyme, Tet(X7). Further, we identify Tet(X7) in a clinical Pseudomonas aeruginosa isolate and demonstrate its contribution to tetracycline resistance. Lastly, we show anhydrotetracycline and semi-synthetic analogues inhibit Tet(X7) to prevent enzymatic tetracycline degradation and increase tetracycline efficacy against strains expressing tet(X7). This work improves our understanding of tet(X)-mediated resistance and provides the foundation for an inhibition-based strategy for countering resistance.

Sequencing Center: National University of Science and Technology
Isolation Comments: isolated from tracheal secretions of a patient with chronic airway colonization
Comments: We performed Illumina whole-genome sequencing on carbapenem resistant Pseudomonas aeruginosa strain isolated from tracheal secretions of a patient with chronic airway colonization. The draft genome comprises of 6,310,348 bp and 5797 CDS, including the metallo beta-lactamase blaVIM-2. The isolate harbors 3 prophage fragments, 18 antibiotic resistance associated genes, and 210 virulence genes.

Comments: We sequenced carbapenem resistant Pseudomonas aeruginosa strain isolated from cystic fibrosis patient with chronic colonization of airways. The draft genome comprises of 6,770,411bp with chromosomally encoded Carbapenemase blaNDM-1 and extended spectrum beta-lactamase blaPME-1.
